question: How do students join the Office Hours live stream if the Zoom link isn’t shared with us?
answer_agent:
Students do **not** use the Zoom link directly.

For Office Hours / live workshop sessions:
- The **Zoom link is only for instructors/presenters/TAs**.
- Students join via **YouTube Live**.
- Questions should be submitted through **Slido** (the link is pinned in chat when the session is live).
- The live stream URL is usually posted in the **announcements channel on Telegram and Slack** before the session starts.
- You can also watch on the **DataTalksClub YouTube channel**.

Avoid posting questions in the chat, since they may be missed.

question: Why can’t I find my name on the leaderboard, and how do I jump to my own entry?
answer_agent:
You’re assigned a random public display name when your account is created, so your real name may not appear on the leaderboard.

To find your own entry:
- Click **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”**

If you want to see or change the name shown publicly:
- Go to **“Edit Course Profile”**
- The **first field** is your public nickname/display name
- The **second field** is your official name for the certificate

If you want, I can also explain the difference between the leaderboard name and certificate name.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: Why isn’t the certificate available for self-paced students?
answer_agent:
The certificate isn’t available for self-paced students because the course requires **live cohort participation** for certification.

According to the FAQ, certification depends on the **peer-review process** for the capstone project: you must review 3 other students’ projects, and that can only happen while the course is actively running. Once the form is closed and the peer-review list is compiled, self-paced students can’t complete that requirement.

So in short: **no live cohort = no certificate**.
